candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line above EBIT, but it is a company-level total and not clearly attributable specifically to the selected COGS bucket 'Operating and maintenance costs'. Adding it to COGS would risk misallocation and double counting.
Operating-section depreciation component, but not clearly tied to COGS or to the selected COGS row. Looks asset-specific rather than cost-bucket-specific.
Large depreciation component under the D&A summary, but still not labelled as in COGS/cost of sales. Bucket attribution to selected COGS row is unclear.
Amortization component likely overhead/admin-related rather than clearly COGS-related, so it should not be added to COGS absent explicit labeling.

variables.notes: Only unallocated operating D&A lines are visible. None is explicitly identified as related to cost of sales / operating and maintenance costs. Conservative treatment is to avoid adding any D&A separately to COGS to prevent misallocation or double counting.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line above EBIT, but it is a total operating D&A amount and not clearly attributable specifically to the selected XSGA bucket 'Staff costs'.
Asset-specific depreciation component, not clearly SG&A-related or embedded specifically in Staff costs.
Depreciation component of the D&A total, but not labelled as SG&A/admin/staff-related. Attribution to XSGA bucket is unclear.
This could plausibly relate to administrative overhead, but the statement does not explicitly tie it to SG&A or the selected XSGA row(s). Using it would be judgmental.

variables.notes: The statement shows only total/components of operating D&A, not bucket-specific SG&A D&A. Because XSGA final_choice is the selected overhead bucket(s) and no row explicitly says depreciation/amortization in SG&A/admin/staff, conservative output is no separate XSGA_DA row.
